summary: Bug fix VERIFIED for What-If Analyzer. The fix in WhatIfView.js (line 35: changed r[idx] to r[feat]) correctly resolves the issue where all features were showing as empty dropdown selects. Now numeric features display range sliders + number inputs, and categorical features display populated dropdown selects.
passed_tests: Login with test credentials (shubhrangshub@gmail.com / MyNewPass123!) - PASS
Load sample dataset (sample-dataset-0 Loan Approval) - PASS
Select target variable using suggested target button - PASS
Train model (waited 18 seconds for completion) - PASS
NUMERIC features show range sliders (16 found) - PASS
NUMERIC features show number inputs (16 found) - PASS
CATEGORICAL features show dropdown selects (LoanPurpose, Education) - PASS
Dropdown selects have populated options (not empty) - PASS
Initial values populated with median (Age: 46.00, Income: 103820.00, etc.) - PASS
Slider modification updates number input (46.00 → 50.14) - PASS
Changed fields highlighted in fuchsia/pink - PASS
critical_code_review_comments: Bug fix in WhatIfView.js line 35 is correct: changed `rows.map(r => r[idx])` to `rows.map(r => r[feat])` because dataProfile.rows stores objects keyed by column name, not arrays indexed by position
The featureStats useMemo now correctly accesses row data by column name (feat) instead of numeric index (idx)
This allows proper detection of numeric vs categorical features based on actual data values
seed_data_creation: None - used existing Loan Approval sample dataset
context_for_next_testing_agent: What-If Analyzer bug fix verified. The fix changed data access from numeric index (r[idx]) to column name (r[feat]) in featureStats useMemo. Loan Approval dataset has 9 numeric columns (Age, Income, LoanAmount, CreditScore, EmploymentYears, DebtToIncome, NumCreditLines, MonthlyPayment, Approved) and 3 categorical columns (HomeOwnership, LoanPurpose, Education). The What-If view correctly shows: (1) range sliders + number inputs for numeric features, (2) populated dropdown selects for categorical features, (3) initial values set to median for numeric and first category for categorical, (4) fuchsia highlighting for changed fields in Modified Scenario panel.

bug_fix_verification.bug_description: All features were showing as empty dropdown selects instead of numeric range+number inputs for numeric columns
bug_fix_verification.root_cause: featureStats useMemo was accessing row data by numeric index (r[idx]) but dataProfile.rows stores objects keyed by column name
bug_fix_verification.fix_applied: Changed r[idx] to r[feat] (column name) in WhatIfView.js line 35
bug_fix_verification.verification_result: VERIFIED - Numeric features now correctly show range sliders + number inputs, categorical features show populated dropdowns
